stem: A recipe for lemonade calls for 3 cups of water for every 2 cups of lemon juice. If Maria wants to make lemonade using 8 cups of lemon juice, how many cups of water will she need?
explanation:
The recipe gives a ratio of water to lemon juice of 3:2. We can set up a proportion to find how much water is needed for 8 cups of lemon juice:

$\frac{3 \text{ cups water}}{2 \text{ cups lemon juice}} = \frac{x \text{ cups water}}{8 \text{ cups lemon juice}}$

Cross-multiplying: $3 \times 8 = 2 \times x$

$24 = 2x$

$x = 12$

Therefore, Maria will need 12 cups of water.

distractorRationale.A: Student may have added 2 to 8 instead of setting up the correct proportion
distractorRationale.B: Correct answer using proper proportional reasoning
distractorRationale.C: Student may have multiplied 2 × 8 instead of using the 3:2 ratio
distractorRationale.D: Student may have multiplied 3 × 8 without considering the proportional relationship
